title: Bruins’ Marat Khusnutdinov has 0 points this postseason. It hasn’t stopped him from playing winning hockey.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins "He did exactly what he did all year long.
And that's why he's playing [on the] first line, right?
" Marat Khusnutdinov has made an impact this playoff series for Boston.

title: Are you or someone you know a Class of 2026 grad with a job already lined up?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Tell Us For many in the Class of 2026, the job search feels more uncertain than it has in years.

Kieley DesRosiers threw her cap alongside her friends and fellow soon to be graduates from Tufts University School of Dental Medicine as they took part in a photo shoot in the Public Garden in Boston, Massachusetts on April 12, 2026.

(Jessica Rinaldi/Globe Staff) By Annie Jonas April 29, 2026 | 4:47 PM 2 minutes to read With commencement ceremonies on the horizon across Massachusetts, a familiar question is echoing among graduates and their families: What comes next?

title: Former Bruins coach Bruce Cassidy predicted David Pastrnak’s OT goal on TNT
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins "We're close.
Heck of a coach, and pretty sure he's gonna be back on the bench in no time.
” Bruce Cassidy coached David Pastrnak in both Boston and Providence.

title: Mass. gambler sues Kalshi seeking repayment for lost bets
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Local News The class-action complaint argues the prediction-market company ran unlicensed sports betting, bolstering the attorney general’s separate push to bar Kalshi in the state.
A phone displays crypto trades on Kalshi on Thursday, April 16, 2026.

(AP Photo/Jenny Kane) A Raynham man who says he struggles with gambling addiction lost tens of thousands of dollars on Kalshi’s sports-betting markets within a single month and is now suing the company in a class-action suit to get that money back.

title: Fidelity to bring employees back to the office 5 days a week
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Local News The company will retain its current offices to support the shift, even as it prepares to move to its new Seaport campus.
Fidelty headquarters on Summer Street.
John Tlumacki/Boston Globe Fidelity is pulling its Boston workforce back to the office full-time in September, marking a major change from the company’s current hybrid work plan.

title: State police rescue hiker from Mount Washington (Massachusetts)
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News A woman got lost while hiking in the Massachusetts town Mount Washington — not New Hampshire's famous mountain.
Massachusetts State Police State police rescued a woman whose cell phone was losing signal after she got lost while hiking in Mount Washington, Mass.
, Sunday evening, authorities said.

title: Tax preparers facing fraud and money laundering charges in alleged years-long scheme in Chelsea
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News The charges stem from a multi-agency investigation of a tax scheme that occurred between April 2017 and July 2021, authorities said.
ShareSend this article to your social connections.

Five people are facing fraud and money laundering charges after prosecutors say they ran a years-long scheme in Chelsea using stolen identities and false tax returns to collect more than $220,000 in refunds.

title: Judge OKs Karen Read’s use of Proctor texts in new civil lawsuit
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Local News Read’s lawyers previously alleged the ex-trooper’s messages were “staggeringly anti-woman, racist, homophobic, [and] antisemitic.
” Karen Read reacted in court as the verdict in her trial was read in June, 2025.
Greg Derr/The Patriot Ledger, Pool, File By Abby Patkin April 29, 2026 | 3:25 PM 1 minute to read ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: Video of person in apparent KKK robe riles R.I. town
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Local News The hooded individual, who appeared to be wearing a white Ku Klux Klan robe, was seen walking around West Warwick.

By Abby Patkin April 29, 2026 | 2:58 PM 1 minute to read A Rhode Island community is on edge after social media videos appeared to show a person in a white Ku Klux Klan robe and hood walking around town in the middle of the night.
The West Warwick Police Department said it is investigating the incident, which happened on Main Street around 2 a.

title: ‘It’s doing exactly what art is supposed to do’: Readers react to MFA nude performance
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Readers Say Many defended Xandra Ibarra’s “Nude Laughing” as provocative, while others questioned whether it belonged at the MFA.
Xandra Ibarra performs her piece, "Nude Laughing," at the MFA.
